questions.question_text: AgCN reacts with haloalkanes to form isocyanide. Haloalkanes react with KCN to form alkyl cyanides as the main product. Why?
questions.question_html: AgCN reacts with haloalkanes to form isocyanide. Haloalkanes react with KCN to form alkyl cyanides as the main product. Why?

options.text: KCN is mainly covalent in nature.
options.html: KCN is mainly covalent in nature.

options.text: AgCN is mainly ionic in nature.
options.html: AgCN is mainly ionic in nature.

options.text: KCN provides cyanide ions in solution.
options.html: KCN provides cyanide ions in solution.

options.text: AgCN provides cyanide ions in solution.
options.html: AgCN provides cyanide ions in solution.

questions.correct_option_text: KCN provides cyanide ions in solution.
questions.explanation: AgCN is covalent in nature while KCN is ionic. So KCN produces CN - in the solution. CN - is an ambient nucleophile i.e. it can form bonds from both C and N atoms. KCN is an ionic compound so both C and N sides are available for the formation of a bond, but the C-C bond is stronger than the C-N bond so cyanide is formed. The AgCN is a covalent compound, so only the N side is available for CN to form bond and thus isocyanide is formed.

questions.question_html: Bromomethane, Chloromethane, Dibromomethane, 1 – Chloropropane, Isopropyl chloride, 1–Chlorobutane all are:
questions.explanation: All these given compounds are covalent in nature. Hence, they are soluble in organic solvents.

questions.question_html: CH3 = CH2 CH3 + H – I {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} CH3 CH2 CH2I + CH3 CHICH3 (major). This reaction is________.
questions.explanation: This is an electrophilic addition reaction following Markovnikov's rule.

questions.question_text: Finkelstein reaction is:
questions.question_html: Finkelstein reaction is:

options.text: CH 3 = CH 2 + H - I {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} CH 3 CH 2 CH 2 I + CH 3 CHICH 3
CH 3 CH 2 CH 2 Cl + NaBr {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} CH 3 CH 2 CH 2 Br + NaCl
CH 3 CH 2 CH 2 Cl + NaI {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} CH 3 CH 2 CH 2 I + NaCl
CH 3 CH 2 CH 2 I + NaCl {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} CH 3 CH 2 CH 2 - Cl + NaI

questions.correct_option_text: CH 3 CH 2 CH 2 Cl + NaI {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} CH 3 CH 2 CH 2 I + NaCl
questions.explanation: Halide exchange reaction is Finkelstein reaction in which alkyl chloride is converted to alkyl iodide. The classic Finkelstein reaction entails the conversion of an alkyl chloride or an alkyl bromide to an alkyl iodide by treatment with a solution of sodium iodide in dry acetone. Sodium iodide is soluble in acetone and sodium chloride and sodium bromide are not. The reaction is driven toward products according to Le chatelier's principle due to the precipitation of the salt insoluble in acetone. For example, in this case, chloropropane can be converted to iodopropane: CH 3 CH 2 CH 2 Cl (acetone) + NaI (acetone) {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} CH 3 CH 2 CH 2 I (acetone) + NaCl (s)

questions.question_text: In the following reaction, the compound used in the reaction for synthesizing ethyl fluoride is: ________ +AgF {tex}\to{/tex} H 3 C – F + AgBr

________ +AgF {tex}\to{/tex} H3C – F + AgBr

options.html: H<sub>3</sub>C – Br,Free radical fluorination of methane

questions.explanation: Alkyl fluorides are only formed by Swartz's reaction. In Swartz reaction, alkyl chlorides or bromide reacts with metallic flouride AgF to form alkyl fluorides.

questions.question_text: Which compound in the following pair reacts faster in S N 2 reaction with OH – ? CH 3 Br or CH 3 CH 3 Cl, (CH 3 ) 3 CCl
questions.question_html:
Which compound in the following pair reacts faster in SN2 reaction with OH–?

  1. CH3Br or CH3
  2. CH3Cl, (CH3)3CCl
questions.explanation: For the S N 2 reaction leaving group should be good and alkyl halide should be primary. So, CH 3 Br and CH 3 Cl are primary organic compounds. Hence the rate of S N 2 reaction will be more.

questions.question_text: Arrange the following organic compounds in descending order of their reactivity towards S N 1 reaction C 6 H 5 CH 2 Br, C 6 H 5 CH(C 6 H 5 )Br, C 6 H 5 CH(CH 3 )Br, C 6 H 5 C(CH 3 )(C 6 H 5 )Br
questions.question_html:
Arrange the following organic compounds in descending order of their reactivity towards SN1 reaction
C6H5CH2Br, C6H5CH(C6H5)Br, C6H5CH(CH3)Br, C6H5C(CH3)(C6H5)Br

options.text: C 6 H 5 C(CH 3 )(C 6 H 5 )Br > C 6 H 5 CH(C 6 H 5 )Br > C 6 H 5 CH(CH 3 )Br > C 6 H 5 CH 2 Br
C 6 H 5 C(CH 3 )(C 6 H 5 )Br > C 6 H 5 CH(C 6 H 2 )Br > C 6 H 5 CH 2 Br > C 6 H 5 CH(CH 3 )Br
C 6 H 5 C(CH 3 )(C 6 H 5 )Br > C 6 H 5 CH(C 6 H 5 )Br > C 6 H 5 CH 2 Br > C 6 H 5 CH(CH 3 )Br
C 6 H 5 C(CH 3 )(C 6 H 5 )Br > C 3 H 5 CH(CH 3 )Br > C 6 H 5 CH 2 Br > C 6 H 5 CH(C 6 H 5 )Br

questions.correct_option_text: C 6 H 5 C(CH 3 )(C 6 H 5 )Br > C 6 H 5 CH(C 6 H 5 )Br > C 6 H 5 CH(CH 3 )Br > C 6 H 5 CH 2 Br
questions.explanation: The nucleophilic substitution in S N 1 occurs in two steps. In step I, the polarised C—Br bond undergoes slow cleavage to produce a carbocation and a bromide ion. This is the slow rate determining step. Thus, the stability of the carbocation formed determines the rate of reaction. The carbocation thus formed is then attacked by nucleophile in step II to complete the substitution reaction. In C 6 H 5 C(CH 3 )(C 6 H 5 )Br carbocation will be tertiary and will be resonance stablised. So the rate of S N 1 reaction is greatest in this case. In case of C 6 H 5 CH(C 6 H 5 )Br, the carbocation formed will be secondary and hence less stable than the previous one, so the reaction will be slower in this case. In C 6 H 5 CH(CH 3 )Br, the carbocation formed will be less stable than that formed in case of C 6 H 5 CH(C 6 H 5 )Br because in the latter the carbocation is stabilised by two phenyl groups due to resonance. In C 6 H 5 CH 2 Br, the carbocation formed will be primary and hence least stable causing the rate of reaction to be least in this case.

questions.question_text: Upon oxidation, one of the following halogen-containing compounds forms an extremely poisonous gas that is: ________ + O 2 {tex}\xrightarrow{{}}{/tex} 2 COCl 2 + 2HCl
questions.question_html:
Upon oxidation, one of the following halogen-containing compounds forms an extremely poisonous gas that is:
________ + O2  {tex}\xrightarrow{{}}{/tex} 2 COCl2 + 2HCl
questions.explanation: CHCl 3 on reaction with oxygen forms phosgene. Chloroform decomposes when it exposed to sunlight. It decomposes to harmful gas phosgene(COCl 2 ).

questions.question_text: Carbon – halogen bond of alkyl halides is responsible for their nucleophilic substitution, elimination, and their reaction with metal atoms to form organometallic compounds because of its:
questions.question_html: Carbon – halogen bond of alkyl halides is responsible for their nucleophilic substitution, elimination, and their reaction with metal atoms to form organometallic compounds because of its:
questions.explanation: Since halogen atoms are more electronegative than carbon, the carbon-halogen bond of alkyl halide is polarised; the carbon atom bears a partial positive charge whereas the halogen atom bears a partial negative charge. This is responsible for nucleophilic substitution reactions, elimination reactions, and the reaction of alkyl halides with a metal to form organometallic compounds.

questions.question_text: A mixture containing two enantiomers in equal proportions:
questions.question_html: A mixture containing two enantiomers in equal proportions:

options.text: will be called a racemic mixture
options.html: will be called a racemic mixture

options.text: will be called a racemic mixture and will have a zero optical rotation.
options.html: will be called a racemic mixture and will have a zero optical rotation.

questions.correct_option_text: will be called a racemic mixture and will have a zero optical rotation.
questions.explanation: Enantiomers are chiral molecules that are mirror images of one another. Furthermore, the molecules are non-superimposable on one another. This means that the molecules cannot be placed on top of one another and give the same arrangement of atoms in space. If they are present in equal proportions, the mixture is called a racemic mixture and it is optically inactive, as one isomer will rotate light in the direction opposite to another.

questions.question_text: {tex}{H_3}C - C{H_2} - CH = CH - C{H_3}\xleftarrow{{ - OH}}{H_3}C - C{H_2} - C{H_2} - \mathop {\mathop {CH}\limits^| }\limits^{Br} - \mathop {\mathop {C{H_2}}\limits_| }\limits_H {/tex} {tex}\xrightarrow{{OH}} - {H_3}C - C{H_2} - C{H_2} - CH = C{H_2}{/tex} In the reaction, 2 – Bromopentane on heating with alcoholic KOH, forms two compounds: Pent – 1 – ene and Pent – 2 – ene. Which one of the following statements is true?
questions.question_html:
{tex}{H_3}C - C{H_2} - CH = CH - C{H_3}\xleftarrow{{ - OH}}{H_3}C - C{H_2} - C{H_2} - \mathop {\mathop {CH}\limits^| }\limits^{Br} - \mathop {\mathop {C{H_2}}\limits_| }\limits_H {/tex}
{tex}\xrightarrow{{OH}} - {H_3}C - C{H_2} - C{H_2} - CH = C{H_2}{/tex}
In the reaction, 2 – Bromopentane on heating with alcoholic KOH, forms two compounds: Pent – 1 – ene and Pent – 2 – ene. Which one of the following statements is true?

options.text: Pent – 2 – ene is formed as a major product
options.html: Pent – 2 – ene is formed as a major product

options.text: Pent – 1 – ene is formed as a major product
options.html: Pent – 1 – ene is formed as a major product

options.text: Sometimes Pent –1 – ene is the major product and other times Pent – 2 – ene
options.html: Sometimes Pent –1 – ene is the major product and other times Pent – 2 – ene

questions.correct_option_text: Pent – 2 – ene is formed as a major product
questions.explanation: Pent-2-ene is the major product as substituted alkene is formed in major quantity according to Zaitsev (Saytzeff's) rule. The process is known as β− elimination as it involves the elimination of β− Hydrogen.

questions.question_text: In the given reaction, {tex}\mathop {{H_3}C - C{H_2} - CH}\limits_{Pent - 2 - ene} = CH - C{H_3}\xleftarrow{{ - OH}}\mathop {{H_3}C - C{H_2} - C{H_2} - \mathop {\mathop {CH}\limits^| }\limits^{Br} - \mathop {\mathop {C{H_2}}\limits_| }\limits_H }\limits_{2 - Bromopen\tan e} {/tex} {tex}\xrightarrow{{ - OH}}\mathop {{H_3}C - C{H_2} - C{H_2} - CH = C{H_2}}\limits_{Pent - 1 - ene} {/tex} 2 – Bromopentane on heating with alcoholic KOH, forms two compounds: Pent – 1 – ene and Pent – 2 – ene. If one major and one minor product are formed, then:
questions.question_html:
In the given reaction,
{tex}\mathop {{H_3}C - C{H_2} - CH}\limits_{Pent - 2 - ene} = CH - C{H_3}\xleftarrow{{ - OH}}\mathop {{H_3}C - C{H_2} - C{H_2} - \mathop {\mathop {CH}\limits^| }\limits^{Br} - \mathop {\mathop {C{H_2}}\limits_| }\limits_H }\limits_{2 - Bromopen\tan e} {/tex}
 {tex}\xrightarrow{{ - OH}}\mathop {{H_3}C - C{H_2} - C{H_2} - CH = C{H_2}}\limits_{Pent - 1 - ene} {/tex}
2 – Bromopentane on heating with alcoholic KOH, forms two compounds: Pent – 1 – ene and Pent – 2 – ene. If one major and one minor product are formed, then:

options.text: the major product formed is Pent-2-ene which has the greater number of alkyl groups attached to the C=C.
options.html: the major product formed is Pent-2-ene which has the greater number of alkyl groups attached to the C=C.

options.text: there is formation of more than one alkenes due to the availability of more than one {tex}\beta{/tex} - hydrogen atoms.
options.html: there is formation of more than one alkenes due to the availability of more than one {tex}\beta{/tex}- hydrogen atoms.

options.text: the minor product formed is Pent-1-ene which has the lesser number of alkyl groups attached to the C=C.
options.html: the minor product formed is Pent-1-ene which has the lesser number of alkyl groups attached to the C=C.

questions.explanation: Zaitsev (also pronounced as Saytzeff) formulated a rule: In dehydrohalogenation reactions, the preferred product is that alkene which has the greater number of alkyl groups attached to the double-bonded carbon atoms. So the major alkene will be the more substituted alkene i.e. Pent-2-ene.

questions.question_text: The dipole moment of chlorobenzene is lower than that of cyclohexyl chloride because
questions.question_html: The dipole moment of chlorobenzene is lower than that of cyclohexyl chloride because

options.text: Cl atom is linked to sp 2 hybridized carbon atom in chlorobenzene
options.html: Cl atom is linked to sp<sup>2</sup> hybridized carbon atom in chlorobenzene

options.text: Cl atom is more electropositive in chlorobenzene
options.html: Cl atom is more electropositive in chlorobenzene

options.text: Cl atom is linked to sp 2 hybridized carbon atom in cyclohexyl chloride
options.html: Cl atom is linked to sp<sup>2</sup> hybridized carbon atom in cyclohexyl chloride

options.text: Cl atom is more electronegative in cyclohexyl chloride
options.html: Cl atom is more electronegative in cyclohexyl chloride

questions.correct_option_text: Cl atom is linked to sp 2 hybridized carbon atom in chlorobenzene
questions.explanation: sp 2 carbon is more electronegative than sp 3 carbon. The dipole moment of chlorobenzene is less than cyclohexyl chloride as in chlorobenzene C of C-Cl bond is sp 2 hybridised where as in cyclohexyl chloride the C of C-Cl bond is sp 3 hybridised. As sp 2 hybridised C has more s-character and is more electronegative than sp 3 hybridised C, the resulting difference in electronegativities of C and Cl in chlorobenzene is less as compared to that in cyclohexyl chloride. So chlorobenzene is less polar than cyclohexyl chloride.

questions.question_text: Alkyl halides are insoluble in water
questions.question_html: Alkyl halides are insoluble in water

options.text: the force of attraction between the alkyl halide and water is weaker and cannot overcome the force of attraction between alkyl halide and alkyl halide as also that of water and water molecules
options.html: the force of attraction between the alkyl halide and water is weaker and cannot overcome the force of attraction between alkyl halide and alkyl halide as also that of water and water molecules

options.text: alkyl halides are non polar compounds
options.html: alkyl halides are non polar compounds

options.text: high energy is released when new attractions are set up between the haloalkane and the water molecules
options.html: high energy is released when new attractions are set up between the haloalkane and the water molecules

options.text: weak hydrogen bonds exist between water molecules
options.html: weak hydrogen bonds exist between water molecules

questions.correct_option_text: the force of attraction between the alkyl halide and water is weaker and cannot overcome the force of attraction between alkyl halide and alkyl halide as also that of water and water molecules
questions.explanation: In alkyl halides, there exist dipole-dipole attractions whereas in water van der Waal's forces or the hydrogen bonding exists. It is difficult for alkyl halides to break these hydrogen bonds, hence making them almost insoluble in water.

questions.question_text: Hydrocarbons are prepared from Grignard reagent by:
questions.question_html: Hydrocarbons are prepared from Grignard reagent by:

options.text: by exposing them to Diethyl ether
options.html: by exposing them to Diethyl ether

questions.explanation: Grignard reagents are highly reactive and react with any source of a proton to give hydrocarbons. Even water, alcohols, amines are sufficiently acidic to convert them to corresponding hydrocarbons. Thus Grignard reagent abstracts acidic hydrogen from H 2 O forming alkane. {tex}RMgX + H_2O \rightarrow RH + Mg(OH)X{/tex}

questions.question_text: Grignard reagent should be prepared under anhydrous conditions because:
questions.question_html: Grignard reagent should be prepared under anhydrous conditions because:

options.text: it can react with any source of protons to give hydrocarbons
options.html: it can react with any source of protons to give hydrocarbons

options.text: the Mg – Halogen bond is essentially ionic
options.html: the Mg – Halogen bond is essentially ionic

options.text: the carbon – Mg bond is covalent but highly polar
options.html: the carbon – Mg bond is covalent but highly polar

questions.explanation: In the Grignard reagent, the carbon-magnesium bond is covalent but highly polar, with carbon pulling electrons from electropositive magnesium; the magnesium halogen bond is essentially ionic. Grignard reagents are highly reactive and react with any source of a proton to give hydrocarbons. Even water, alcohols, amines are sufficiently acidic to convert them to corresponding hydrocarbons. {tex}RMgX + H_2O \rightarrow RH + Mg(OH)X{/tex} It is therefore necessary to avoid even traces of moisture from a Grignard reagent.

questions.question_html: Name the following compound as per the IUPAC system

questions.question_text: Name the following aryl halide as per the IUPAC system
questions.question_html: Name the following aryl halide as per the IUPAC system
questions.explanation: Here we have selected the benzene ring as the parent compound with chloro group at position 1 and 2-methylpropyl group present at the position 4. Here halogen has been numbered in preference to the alkyl substituent. Thus the correct IUPAC name would be 1-chloro-4-(2-methylpropyl) benzene.

questions.question_text: Isomers of the compound C 4 H 9 Br are:
questions.question_html: Isomers of the compound C4H9Br are:

options.text: 1 – Bromobutane, 2 – Bromobutane, 1 – bromo- 2 – methylpropane, and 2 – bromo-2 – methylpropane
options.html: 1 – Bromobutane, 2 – Bromobutane, 1 – bromo- 2 – methylpropane, and 2 – bromo-2 – methylpropane

questions.correct_option_text: 1 – Bromobutane, 2 – Bromobutane, 1 – bromo- 2 – methylpropane, and 2 – bromo-2 – methylpropane
questions.explanation: Isomers are compounds having the same molecular formula but different structures. In the given straight-chain 4-C compounds, Br is placed at different positions but the molecular formula is same. {tex}\mathop {C{H_3}C{H_2}CH(Br)C{H_3}}\limits_{2 - Bromobu\tan e} ,\;\mathop {C{H_3}C{H_2}C{H_2}C{H_2}Br}\limits_{1 - Bromobu\tan e} {/tex} In the given branched 4-C compounds, there is a branching of -CH 3 at carbon 2 but Br is placed at positions 1 and 2 respectively. {tex}\mathop {C{H_3}CH(C{H_3})C{H_2}Br}\limits_{1 - Bromo - 2 - methylpropane} ,\;\mathop {C{H_3}C(Br)(C{H_3})C{H_3}}\limits_{2 - Bromo - 2 - methylpropane} {/tex} So, all of these are isomers since they have the same molecular formula but different structures.

questions.question_text: Hydrocarbons having double the number of carbon atoms than present in the original alkyl halide are produced by using:
questions.question_html: Hydrocarbons having double the number of carbon atoms than present in the original alkyl halide are produced by using:
questions.explanation: The Wurtz reaction , named after Charles-Adolphe Wurtz , is a coupling reaction in organic chemistry, organometallic chemistry and recently inorganic main group polymers, whereby two alkyl halides are reacted with sodium metal in dry ethereal solution to form a higher alkane: 2R - X + 2Na {tex} \to {/tex} R - R + 2NaX

questions.question_text: To prepare alkanes containing odd number of carbon atoms, Wurtz reaction is not preferred because:
questions.question_html: To prepare alkanes containing odd number of carbon atoms, Wurtz reaction is not preferred because:

options.text: a mixture of three different alkyl halides has to be used.
options.html: a mixture of three different alkyl halides has to be used.

options.text: a lot of reaction mixture goes wasted.
options.html: a lot of reaction mixture goes wasted.

options.text: a mixture of four different alkyl halides has to be used.
options.html: a mixture of four different alkyl halides has to be used.

options.text: a mixture of two different alkyl halides has to be used.
options.html: a mixture of two different alkyl halides has to be used.

questions.correct_option_text: a mixture of two different alkyl halides has to be used.
questions.explanation: Alkyl halides on treatment with sodium metal in dry ethereal (free from moisture) solution give higher alkanes. This reaction is known as the Wurtz reaction and is used for the preparation of higher alkanes containing even a number of carbon atoms. Many side products are formed when two different alkyl halides are used. So this method is not preferred to prepare alkanes having an odd number of C atoms.

options.text: sp 2 -hybridized carbon atom next to an aromatic ring.
options.html: sp<sup>2</sup>-hybridized carbon atom next to an aromatic ring.

options.text: sp 3 -hybridized carbon atom next to carbon-carbon double bond.
options.html: sp<sup>3</sup>-hybridized carbon atom next to carbon-carbon double bond.

questions.correct_option_text: sp 3 -hybridized carbon atom, next to an aromatic ring bonded to a halogen.
questions.explanation: Benzylic halides are the compounds in which the halogen atom is bonded to an sp 3 -hybridized carbon atom next to an aromatic ring. For example C 6 H 5 CH 2 Cl.

questions.question_text: A halogen used in potential blood substitutes in surgery is:
questions.question_html: A halogen used in potential blood substitutes in surgery is:
questions.explanation: Certain fully fluorinated compounds are being considered as potential blood substitutes in surgery. So fluorine is the element used in these blood substitutes.

questions.explanation: p,p’– Dichlorodiphenyltrichloroethane ( DDT ) , the first chlorinated organic insecticides, was originally prepared in 1873

questions.question_text: Tetrachloromethane (Carbon tetrachloride) is a:
questions.question_html: Tetrachloromethane (Carbon tetrachloride) is a:
questions.explanation: CCl 4 was widely used as a cleaning fluid in industry as a degreasing agent.

questions.question_text: Triiodomethane (Iodoform) is:
questions.question_html: Triiodomethane (Iodoform) is:
questions.explanation: The compound finds small scale use as a disinfectant. Around the beginning of the 20th century, it was used in medicine as a healing and antiseptic dressing for wounds and sores, although this use is now superseded by superior antiseptics.

questions.question_text: The most common freons in industrial use is manufactured by_______.
questions.question_html: The most common freons in industrial use is manufactured by_______.
questions.explanation: The chlorofluorocarbon compounds of methane and ethane are collectively known as freons. They are extremely stable, unreactive, non-toxic, non-corrosive, and easily liquefiable gases. Freon 12 (CCl 2 F 2 ) is one of the most common freons in industrial use. It is manufactured from tetrachloromethane by Swarts reaction.

questions.question_text: Dichloromethane (Methylene chloride) is______.
questions.question_html: Dichloromethane (Methylene chloride) is______.
questions.explanation: Methylene chloride is an organic compound with the formula CH 2 Cl 2 . This colorless, volatile liquid with a moderately sweet aroma is widely used as a solvent, as a paint remover, as a propellant in aerosols, and as a process solvent in the manufacture of drugs. It is also used as a metal cleaning and finishing solvent. Methylene chloride harms the human central nervous system.

questions.question_text: Freon can:
questions.question_html: Freon can:

options.text: both initiate radical chain reactions in the stratosphere and disturb natural ozone balance.
options.html: both initiate radical chain reactions in the stratosphere and disturb natural ozone balance.

options.text: initiate radical chain reactions in the stratosphere.
options.html: initiate radical chain reactions in the stratosphere.

questions.correct_option_text: both initiate radical chain reactions in the stratosphere and disturb natural ozone balance.
questions.explanation: Most freon, even that used in refrigeration, eventually makes its way into the atmosphere where it diffuses unchanged into the stratosphere. In the stratosphere, freon is able to initiate radical chain reactions that can upset the natural ozone balance.

questions.question_text: Chloroform is stored in closed dark coloured bottles completely filled because it:
questions.question_html: Chloroform is stored in closed dark coloured bottles completely filled because it:

options.text: forms an extremely poisonous gas in the presence of light.
options.html: forms an extremely poisonous gas in the presence of light.

options.text: can change its colour in the presence of light and get spoilt by the action of light.
options.html: can change its colour in the presence of light and get spoilt by the action of light.

options.text: gets slowly oxidized by air in the presence of light and form a poisonous gas.
options.html: gets slowly oxidized by air in the presence of light and form a poisonous gas.

options.text: gets slowly oxidized by air in the presence of light.
options.html: gets slowly oxidized by air in the presence of light.

questions.correct_option_text: gets slowly oxidized by air in the presence of light and form a poisonous gas.
questions.explanation: In the presence of light chloroform slowly oxidizes in air to form phosgene (carbonyl chloride COCl 2 ), which is a poisonous gas. It is therefore stored in closed dark coloured bottles completely filled so that air is kept out. 2CHCl 3 + O 2 {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} 2COCl 2 + 2HCl

questions.question_text: Anisole reacts with a mixture of concentrated sulphuric and nitric acids gives
questions.question_html: Anisole reacts with a mixture of concentrated sulphuric and nitric acids gives
questions.explanation: With a mixture of conc.HNO3 and conc. H2SO4 (nitrating mixture), anisole gives ortho and para nitro anisole.

questions.question_text: Carbon tetrachloride has a dipole moment:
questions.question_html: Carbon tetrachloride has a dipole moment:
questions.explanation: CCl 4 is a symmetrical molecule. Hence, the dipole moment is zero.

questions.question_text: A hydrocarbon C 5 H 10 does not react with chlorine in dark but gives a single monochloro compound C 5 H 9 Cl in bright sunlight. The hydrocarbon is:
questions.question_html: A hydrocarbon C5H10 does not react with chlorine in dark but gives a single monochloro compound C5H9Cl in bright sunlight. The hydrocarbon is:
questions.explanation: Alkanes undergo free radical halogenation in the presence of sunlight to give a mixture of isomeric mono- and polyhaloalkanes. So cyclopentane must be the compound with formula C 5 H 10 which must have reacted with Chlorine in presence of sunlight (but not in dark) to form C 5 H 9 Cl.

questions.question_text: Liver when chronically exposed to chloroform gets damaged because:
questions.question_html: Liver when chronically exposed to chloroform gets damaged because:

options.text: chloroform gets converted to carbonyl chloride which is a poison.
options.html: chloroform gets converted to carbonyl chloride which is a poison.

options.text: chloroform depresses the central nervous system.
options.html: chloroform depresses the central nervous system.

options.text: chloroform is not metabolized in the liver.
options.html: chloroform is not metabolized in the liver.

options.text: chloroform develops sores in the liver.
options.html: chloroform develops sores in the liver.

questions.correct_option_text: chloroform gets converted to carbonyl chloride which is a poison.
questions.explanation: Chronic chloroform exposure may cause damage to the liver (where chloroform is metabolized to phosgene) CHCl 3 + O 2 {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} 2COCl 2 + 2HCl

questions.question_text: Which branched chain isomer of the hydrocarbon with molecular mass 72u gives only one isomer of monosubstituted alkyl halide?
questions.question_html: Which branched chain isomer of the hydrocarbon with molecular mass 72u gives only one isomer of monosubstituted alkyl halide?
questions.explanation: Neopentane has all same type of hydrogen and has molecular weight 72u.

questions.question_text: A dibromo derivative of an alkane reacts with sodium metal to form an alicyclic hydrocarbon. The derivative is
questions.question_html: A dibromo derivative of an alkane reacts with sodium metal to form an alicyclic hydrocarbon. The derivative is
questions.explanation: Of all the given options, it is possible with 1,4-dibromobutane to form cyclobutane as shown by intramolecular wurtz reaction.

questions.question_text: The synthesis of 3 – octyne is achieved by adding a bromoalkane into a mixture of sodium amide and an alkyne. The bromoalkane and alkyne respectively are
questions.question_html: The synthesis of 3 – octyne is achieved by adding a bromoalkane into a mixture of sodium amide and an alkyne. The bromoalkane and alkyne respectively are

options.text: BrCH 2 CH 2 CH 2 CH 3 and CH 3 C {tex}\equiv{/tex} CH
BrCH 2 CH 2 CH 3 and CH 3 CH 2 CH 2 C {tex}\equiv{/tex} CH
BrCH 2 CH 2 CH 2 CH 3 and CH 3 CH 2 C {tex}\equiv{/tex} CH
BrCH 2 CH 2 CH 2 CH 2 CH 3 and CH 3 CH 2 C {tex}\equiv{/tex} CH

questions.correct_option_text: BrCH 2 CH 2 CH 2 CH 3 and CH 3 CH 2 C {tex}\equiv{/tex} CH
questions.explanation: CH 3 CH 2 C {tex} \equiv {/tex} BrCH 2 CH 2 CH 2 CH3+NaNH2 {tex} \to {/tex} CH3CH 2 CH 2 CH 2 CH 2 CH 2 C {tex} \equiv {/tex} CH+NH 3 +NaBr NaNH 2 is a very strong base it will abstract H from alkyne (But-1-yne in this case) and then nucleophilic substitution reaction will occur as shown where the resulting compound will be Oct-1-yne as here the reactants contain a total of 8 C.

questions.question_text: The iodine-containing hormone produced by our body is:
questions.question_html: The iodine-containing hormone produced by our body is:
questions.explanation: Our body produces iodine-containing hormone, thyroxine, the deficiency of which causes a disease called a goiter.

questions.question_text: Decomposition of benzene diozonium chloride by using Cu 2 Cl 2 /HCl to form chlorobenzene is:
questions.question_html: Decomposition of benzene diozonium chloride by using Cu2Cl2/HCl to form chlorobenzene is:
questions.explanation: {tex}{C_6}{H_5}N_2^ + C{l^ - } + C{u_2}C{l_2}/HCl \to {C_6}{H_5}Cl{/tex} Mixing the solution of the freshly prepared diazonium salt with cuprous chloride or cuprous bromide results in the replacement of the diazonium group by –Cl or –Br. This is called Sandmeyer's reaction.

questions.question_text: IUPAC name of (CH 3 ) 3 CCl is:
questions.question_html: IUPAC name of (CH3)3CCl is:
questions.explanation: Longest chain will be of three carbon to which Cl and CH 3 will be attached at 2 positions.

questions.question_text: Maximum number of molecules of CH 3 I that can react with a molecule of CH 3 NH 2 is:
questions.question_html: Maximum number of molecules of CH3I that can react with a molecule of CH3NH2 is:
questions.explanation: CH 3 NH 2 can act as a nucleophile to cause substitution reaction. (CH 3 ) 4 N + this is formed when 3 moles of CH 3 NH 2 reacts with methyl halide.

questions.question_text: The best method for the conversion of an alcohol into an alkyl chloride is by treating the alcohol with:
questions.question_html: The best method for the conversion of an alcohol into an alkyl chloride is by treating the alcohol with:

options.text: Dry HCl in the presence of anhydrous ZnCl 2
options.html: Dry HCl in the presence of anhydrous ZnCl<sub>2</sub>

questions.explanation: The hydroxyl group of an alcohol is replaced by halogen on reaction with concentrated halogen acids, phosphorus halides, or thionyl chloride. Thionyl chloride (SOCl 2 ) is preferred because the other two products SO 2 and HCl are escapable gases. Hence, the reaction gives pure alkyl halides. ROH + SOCl 2 {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} RCl + SO 2 (g) + HCl (g)

questions.question_text: Which one of the following forms propane nitrile as the major product?
questions.question_html: Which one of the following forms propane nitrile as the major product?
questions.explanation: CH 3 CH 2 Br + KCN {tex} \to {/tex} CH 3 CH 2 CN + KBr

questions.question_text: Which one of the following is employed as antityphoid drug?
questions.question_html: Which one of the following is employed as antityphoid drug?
questions.explanation: Chlorine containing antibiotics, chloramphenicol, produced by soil microorganisms is very effective for the treatment of typhoid fever. Chloramphenicol is an antityphoid drug.

questions.question_text: Which one of the following is a synthetic halogen compound?
questions.question_html: Which one of the following is a synthetic halogen compound?
questions.explanation: Chloroquine is a synthetic halogen compound. It is used for the treatment of malaria.

questions.question_text: Decreasing order of reactivity of hydrogen halide acids in the conversion of ROH {tex} \to {/tex} RX is:
questions.question_html: Decreasing order of reactivity of hydrogen halide acids in the conversion of ROH {tex} \to {/tex} RX is:
questions.explanation: As we move down the group 17, the size of atom increases as F<Cl<Br<I. Thus the bond strength of hydrogen halides reduces as HF>HCl>HBr>HI. So, it is easiest to break the H-I bond. Hence, the decreasing order of reactivity for the conversion of ROH to RX is HI>HBr>HCl>HF.

questions.question_text: Ethyl benzene cannot be prepared by ______.
questions.question_html: Ethyl benzene cannot be prepared by ______.
questions.explanation: Alkyl halides react with sodium in dry ether to give hydrocarbons containing double the number of carbon atoms present in the halide. This reaction is known as the Wurtz reaction. 2RX + 2Na {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} R-R + 2NaX So, C 6 H 5 CH 2 CH 3 is not prepared by the Wurtz reaction.

questions.question_text: Methyl bromide is converted into ethane by heating it in ether medium with ________.
questions.question_html: Methyl bromide is converted into ethane by heating it in ether medium with ________.
questions.explanation: 2CH 3 Br + 2Na {tex} \to {/tex} CH 3 CH 3 + 2NaBr

questions.question_text: Vinylic halides contain:
questions.question_html: Vinylic halides contain:

options.text: a sp 2 -hybridized carbon atom of an aromatic ring which is bonded to a halogen.
options.html: a sp<sup>2</sup>-hybridized carbon atom of an aromatic ring which is bonded to a halogen.

options.text: a halogen atom bonded to an sp 2 -hybridized carbon atom of a carbon-carbon double bond.
options.html: a halogen atom bonded to an sp<sup>2</sup>-hybridized carbon atom of a carbon-carbon double bond.

options.text: a sp 3 -hybridized carbon atom, next to an aromatic ring, to which halogen atom is bonded.
options.html: a sp<sup>3</sup>-hybridized carbon atom, next to an aromatic ring, to which halogen atom is bonded.

options.text: a halogen atom bonded to an sp 3 -hybridized carbon atom next to carbon-carbon double bond.
options.html: a halogen atom bonded to an sp<sup>3</sup>-hybridized carbon atom next to carbon-carbon double bond.

questions.correct_option_text: a halogen atom bonded to an sp 2 -hybridized carbon atom of a carbon-carbon double bond.
questions.explanation: Vinylic halides are the compounds in which the halogen atom is bonded to an sp 2 -hybridized carbon atom of a carbon-carbon double bond (C = C). For example CH 2 =CHBr.

questions.question_text: p – Dichlorobenzene has ________ and ________ than those of o – and m – isomers.
questions.question_html: p – Dichlorobenzene has ________  and ________ than those of o – and m – isomers.

options.text: higher melting point and higher solubility
options.html: higher melting point and higher solubility

options.text: low melting point and low solubility
options.html: low melting point and low solubility

options.text: lower melting point and higher solubility
options.html: lower melting point and higher solubility

options.text: higher melting point and lower solubility
options.html: higher melting point and lower solubility

questions.correct_option_text: higher melting point and lower solubility
questions.explanation: The para-isomers of dihalobenzenes are high melting as compared to their ortho- and meta-isomers. It is due to the symmetry of para-isomers that fits in crystal lattice better as compared to ortho- and meta-isomers. These compounds have a lower solubility in water but higher solubility in organic solvents.
